16: pub struct McpServer {
17:     engine: Arc<Engine>,
18:     data_dir: std::path::PathBuf,
19: }

21: impl McpServer {
22:     pub fn new(engine: Engine, data_dir: std::path::PathBuf) -> Self {
23:         Self { engine: Arc::new(engine), data_dir }
24:     }
25: 
26:     pub async fn serve_stdio(self) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
27:         let service = self.serve(stdio()).await?;
28:         service.waiting().await?;
29:         Ok(())
30:     }
31: }

33: fn ok_json(value: serde_json::Value) -> StdResult<CallToolResult, ErrorData> {
34:     Ok(CallToolResult::success(vec![Content::text(serde_json::to_string_pretty(&value).unwrap_or_default())]))
35: }
36: 
37: fn err_json(msg: &str) -> StdResult<CallToolResult, ErrorData> {
38:     Ok(CallToolResult::error(vec![Content::text(serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({"error": msg})).unwrap_or_default())]))
39: }
40: 
41: fn parse_args<T: serde::de::DeserializeOwned>(request: &CallToolRequestParams) -> StdResult<T, ErrorData> {
42:     let val = request.arguments.as_ref()
43:         .map(|m| serde_json::Value::Object(m.clone()))
44:         .unwrap_or(serde_json::Value::Object(serde_json::Map::new()));
45:     serde_json::from_value(val)
46:         .map_err(|e| ErrorData::new(ErrorCode(-32602), format!("Invalid params: {e}"), None))
47: }
48: 
49: async fn handle_module_create(engine: &Engine, path: &str, source: &str, version: Option<&str>, summary: Option<&str>, depends_on: &[String], timeout_ms: Option<u64>) -> StdResult<CallToolResult, ErrorData> {
50:     let mut manifest = AutomationManifest::default();
51:     manifest.name = path.to_string();
52:     manifest.version = version.unwrap_or("0.1.0").to_string();
53:     manifest.summary = summary.map(|s| s.to_string());
54:     manifest.timeout_ms = timeout_ms.unwrap_or(30_000);
55:     manifest.depends_on = depends_on.iter().map(|d| DepRef::new(d)).collect();
56:     match engine.backend().register_module(path, source, &manifest).await {
57:         Ok(id) => {
58:             let mut props = HashMap::new();
59:             props.insert("path".into(), serde_json::json!(path));
60:             let _ = engine.graph().add_node(NodeKind::Module, path, props);
61:             ok_json(serde_json::json!({"status":"created","path":path,"hash":id.hash.as_str(),"version":manifest.version}))
62:         }
63:         Err(e) => err_json(&e.to_string()),
64:     }
65: }
66: 
67: fn add_tool(tools: &mut Vec<Tool>, name: &'static str, desc: &'static str, schema: impl Into<Arc<JsonObject>>) {
68:     tools.push(Tool::new(name, desc, schema));
69: }

71: impl ServerHandler for McpServer {
72:     fn get_info(&self) -> ServerInfo {
73:         let mut info = ServerInfo::new(ServerCapabilities::builder().enable_tools().build());
74:         info.instructions = Some("Automaton MCP Server — AI-agent-native Rust automation substrate.".into());
75:         info
76:     }
77: 
78:     fn call_tool(
79:         &self,
80:         request: CallToolRequestParams,
81:         _context: rmcp::service::RequestContext<rmcp::RoleServer>,
82:     ) -> std::pin::Pin<Box<dyn std::future::Future<Output = StdResult<CallToolResult, ErrorData>> + Send + '_>> {
83:         let engine = self.engine.clone();
84:         Box::pin(async move {
85:             match request.name.as_ref() {
86:                 // ── Module Tools ──
87:                 "module_create" => {
88:                     let p: ModuleCreateParams = parse_args(&request)?;
89:                     return handle_module_create(&engine, &p.path, &p.source, p.version.as_deref(), p.summary.as_deref(), &p.depends_on.unwrap_or_default(), p.timeout_ms).await;
90:                 }
91:                 "module_build" => {
92:                     let p: ModuleBuildParams = parse_args(&request)?;
93:                     let module = match engine.backend().get_module(&p.path).await { Ok(Some(m)) => m, _ => return err_json("Module not found") };
94:                     let bc = automaton_build::BuildCache::new(std::path::Path::new(&self.data_dir));
95:                     match bc.build_rust(&p.path, &module.source, &module.manifest) {
96:                         Ok((hash, _path)) => {
97:                             let _ = engine.backend().mark_built(&p.path).await;
98:                             ok_json(serde_json::json!({"status":"built","path":p.path,"hash":hash}))
99:                         }
100:                         Err(e) => err_json(&format!("Build failed: {e}")),
101:                     }
102:                 }
103:                 "module_validate" => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"valid":true})),
104:                 "module_run" => {
105:                     let p: ModuleRunParams = parse_args(&request)?;
106:                     let binary = engine.backend().build_cache_dir().join(p.path.replace('.', "_"));
107:                     if !binary.exists() {
108:                         return err_json("not built yet");
109:                     }
110:                     let rt = Runtime::new(RuntimeConfig {
111:                         work_dir: self.data_dir.join("work"),
112:                         temp_dir: self.data_dir.join("tmp"),
113:                         ..Default::default()
114:                     });
115:                     let input = p.input.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({}));
116:                     match rt.run_binary(&binary, &input, 30000).await {
117:                         Ok(output) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"status":"completed","output":output})),
118:                         Err(e) => err_json(&e.to_string()),
119:                     }
120:                 }
121:                 "module_deprecate" => {
122:                     let p: ModuleDeprecateParams = parse_args(&request)?;
123:                     let _ = engine.graph().delete_node(&p.path);
124:                     ok_json(serde_json::json!({"status":"deprecated","path":p.path}))
125:                 }
126:                 "module_search" => {
127:                     let p: ModuleSearchParams = parse_args(&request)?;
128:                     let all = engine.backend().list_modules().await.unwrap_or_default();
129:                     let limit = p.limit.unwrap_or(20);
130:                     let results: Vec<_> = all.iter()
131:                         .filter(|(path, _, _, _)| path.contains(&p.query))
132:                         .take(limit)
133:                         .map(|(p, v, h, b)| serde_json::json!({"path":p,"version":v,"hash":h,"built":b}))
134:                         .collect();
135:                     ok_json(serde_json::json!({"count":results.len(),"results":results}))
136:                 }
137:                 "module_template" => {
138:                     let p: ModuleTemplateParams = parse_args(&request)?;
139:                     let tmpl = automaton_build::templates::get_template(&p.pattern);
140:                     let source = tmpl.map(|t| t.source.to_string()).unwrap_or_else(|| {
141:                         let safe = p.path.replace('.', "_");
142:                         format!("fn main() {{ println!(\"{{}}\", serde_json::json!({{ \"status\":\"ok\",\"module\":\"{safe}\" }})); }}\n")
143:                     });
144:                     let mut manifest = AutomationManifest::default();
145:                     manifest.name = p.path.clone();
146:                     manifest.summary = p.description;
147:                     let _ = engine.backend().register_module(&p.path, &source, &manifest).await;
148:                     ok_json(serde_json::json!({"status":"created","path":p.path,"pattern":p.pattern,"source_len":source.len()}))
149:                 }
150:                 "module_list_templates" => {
151:                     let templates = automaton_build::templates::all_templates();
152:                     let list: Vec<serde_json::Value> = templates.iter().map(|t| {
153:                         serde_json::json!({
154:                             "name": t.name,
155:                             "description": t.description,
156:                         })
157:                     }).collect();
158:                     ok_json(serde_json::json!({"templates": list, "count": list.len()}))
159:                 }
160: 
161:                 // ── Workflow Tools ──
162:                 "workflow_plan" => {
163:                     let p: WorkflowPlanParams = parse_args(&request)?;
164:                     let opts = PlanOptions { max_depth: p.max_depth.unwrap_or(10), ..Default::default() };
165:                     match engine.plan(&p.start, &opts).await {
166:                         Ok(rg) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"run_graph_id":rg.id,"workflow":rg.workflow_name,"modules":rg.modules.len()})),
167:                         Err(e) => err_json(&e.to_string()),
168:                     }
169:                 }
170:                 "workflow_materialize" => {
171:                     let p: WorkflowPlanParams = parse_args(&request)?;
172:                     let opts = PlanOptions { max_depth: p.max_depth.unwrap_or(10), dry_run: true, ..Default::default() };
173:                     match engine.plan(&p.start, &opts).await { Ok(rg) => match engine.materialize(&rg) {
174:                         Ok(_) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"status":"valid_dag"})),
175:                         Err(e) => err_json(&format!("Invalid DAG: {e}")),
176:                     }, Err(e) => err_json(&e.to_string()) }
177:                 }
178: 
179:                 // ── Graph Tools ──
180:                 "graph_query" => {
181:                     let p: GraphQueryParams = parse_args(&request)?;
182:                     let nodes = match p.kind.as_deref().unwrap_or("") {
183:                         "" => engine.graph().all_nodes().unwrap_or_default(),
184:                         "module" => engine.graph().find_nodes_by_kind(NodeKind::Module).unwrap_or_default(),
185:                         "workflow" => engine.graph().find_nodes_by_kind(NodeKind::Workflow).unwrap_or_default(),
186:                         "trigger" => engine.graph().find_nodes_by_kind(NodeKind::Trigger).unwrap_or_default(),
187:                         "resource" => engine.graph().find_nodes_by_kind(NodeKind::Resource).unwrap_or_default(),
188:                         k => return err_json(&format!("Unknown kind: {k}")),
189:                     };
190:                     ok_json(serde_json::json!({"count":nodes.len(),"nodes":nodes}))
191:                 }
192:                 "graph_pathfind" => {
193:                     let p: GraphPathfindParams = parse_args(&request)?;
194:                     let paths = engine.graph().find_path(&p.from, &p.to).unwrap_or_default();
195:                     ok_json(serde_json::json!({"paths_found":paths.len()}))
196:                 }
197:                 "graph_add_edge" => {
198:                     let p: GraphAddEdgeParams = parse_args(&request)?;
199:                     let ek = match p.kind.to_uppercase().as_str() {
200:                         "DEPENDS_ON" => EdgeKind::DependsOn, "CALLS" => EdgeKind::Calls,
201:                         "TRIGGERS" => EdgeKind::Triggers, "USES_RESOURCE" => EdgeKind::UsesResource,
202:                         "EMITS" => EdgeKind::Emits, "CONSUMES" => EdgeKind::Consumes,
203:                         "BLOCKED_BY" => EdgeKind::BlockedBy, "ALTERNATIVE_TO" => EdgeKind::AlternativeTo,
204:                         "UPGRADES" => EdgeKind::Upgrades, "DERIVED_FROM" => EdgeKind::DerivedFrom,
205:                         _ => return err_json("Unknown edge kind"),
206:                     };
207:                     match engine.graph().add_edge(&p.source, &p.target, ek, HashMap::new()) {
208:                         Ok(eid) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"id":eid})),
209:                         Err(e) => err_json(&e.to_string()),
210:                     }
211:                 }
212: 
213:                 // ── Flow Tools ──
214:                 "flow_create" => {
215:                     let p: FlowCreateParams = parse_args(&request)?;
216:                     let steps: Vec<FlowStep> = serde_json::from_value(p.steps).unwrap_or_default();
217:                     let def = FlowDefinition {
218:                         path: p.path.clone(),
219:                         steps,
220:                         summary: p.summary.clone(),
221:                         on_failure: p.on_failure.clone(),
222:                         ..Default::default()
223:                     };
224:                     let flat = FlowEngine::flatten(&def);
225:                     match flat {
226:                         Ok(s) => {
227:                             // Persist the flow
228:                             let def_json = serde_json::to_value(&def).unwrap_or_default();
229:                             match engine.backend().store_flow(
230:                                 &p.path, "0.1.0", &def_json,
231:                                 p.summary.as_deref(), p.on_failure.as_deref(),
232:                             ).await {
233:                                 Ok(id) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({
234:                                     "status":"flow_created","flow_id":id,
235:                                     "path":p.path,"steps":s.len()
236:                                 })),
237:                                 Err(e) => err_json(&e.to_string()),
238:                             }
239:                         }
240:                         Err(e) => err_json(&e.to_string()),
241:                     }
242:                 }
243:                 "flow_show" => {
244:                     let p: FlowShowParams = parse_args(&request)?;
245:                     match engine.backend().get_flow(&p.path).await {
246:                         Ok(Some(flow)) => ok_json(flow),
247:                         Ok(None) => err_json("Flow not found"),
248:                         Err(e) => err_json(&e.to_string()),
249:                     }
250:                 }
251:                 "flow_list" => {
252:                     match engine.backend().list_flows().await {
253:                         Ok(flows) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"flows": flows})),
254:                         Err(e) => err_json(&e.to_string()),
255:                     }
256:                 }
257:                 "flow_delete" => {
258:                     let p: FlowShowParams = parse_args(&request)?;
259:                     match engine.backend().delete_flow(&p.path).await {
260:                         Ok(_) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"status":"deleted","path":p.path})),
261:                         Err(e) => err_json(&e.to_string()),
262:                     }
263:                 }
264:                 "flow_execute" => {
265:                     let p: FlowExecuteParams = parse_args(&request)?;
266:                     // Try flow persistence path first
267:                     let flow_result = engine.backend().get_flow(&p.path).await;
268:                     match flow_result {
269:                         Ok(Some(flow)) => {
270:                             // Execute persisted flow via FlowEngine with Runtime
271:                             let def_val = flow.get("definition").cloned().unwrap_or_default();
272:                             if let Ok(def) = serde_json::from_value::<FlowDefinition>(def_val) {
273:                                 match FlowEngine::flatten(&def) {
274:                                     Ok(steps) => {
275:                                         let rt = Runtime::new(RuntimeConfig {
276:                                             work_dir: self.data_dir.join("work"),
277:                                             temp_dir: self.data_dir.join("tmp"),
278:                                             ..Default::default()
279:                                         });
280:                                         let bc = engine.backend().build_cache_dir().to_path_buf();
281:                                         match FlowEngine::execute(
282:                                             &steps,
283:                                             Some(engine.backend()),
284:                                             &rt,
285:                                             &bc,
286:                                         ).await {
287:                                             Ok(outputs) => {
288:                                                 let results: serde_json::Map<_, _> = outputs.into_iter().collect();
289:                                                 ok_json(serde_json::json!({"status":"completed","mode":"flow","results":results}))
290:                                             }
291:                                             Err(e) => err_json(&e.to_string())
292:                                         }
293:                                     }
294:                                     Err(e) => err_json(&e.to_string())
295:                                 }
296:                             } else {
297:                                 err_json("Invalid flow definition")
298:                             }
299:                         }
300:                         _ => {
301:                             // Fall back to Engine DAG execution (module-based)
302:                             let opts = PlanOptions { ..Default::default() };
303:                             match engine.plan(&p.path, &opts).await {
304:                                 Ok(rg) => {
305:                                     match engine.materialize(&rg) {
306:                                         Ok(dag) => {
307:                                             let result = engine.execute(dag).await;
308:                                             match result {
309:                                                 Ok(output) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"status":"completed","mode":"dag","run_id":output.run_graph_id,"results":output.results})),
310:                                                 Err(e) => err_json(&e.to_string()),
311:                                             }
312:                                         }
313:                                         Err(e) => err_json(&format!("Invalid DAG: {e}")),
314:                                     }
315:                                 }
316:                                 Err(e) => err_json(&e.to_string()),
317:                             }
318:                         }
319:                     }
320:                 }
321: 
322:                 // ── Schedule Tools ──
323:                 "schedule_create" => {
324:                     let p: ScheduleCreateParams = parse_args(&request)?;
325:                     match Scheduler::validate(&p.schedule) {
326:                         Ok(_) => {
327:                             let config = serde_json::json!({"schedule": p.schedule, "args": p.args});
328:                             match engine.backend().create_trigger(&p.target_path, false, "cron", &config).await {
329:                                 Ok(id) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"status":"schedule_created","id":id,"target":p.target_path,"schedule":p.schedule,"valid_cron":true})),
330:                                 Err(e) => err_json(&e.to_string()),
331:                             }
332:                         }
333:                         Err(e) => err_json(&e.to_string()),
334:                     }
335:                 }
336:                 "schedule_validate" => {
337:                     let p: ScheduleValidateParams = parse_args(&request)?;
338:                     match Scheduler::validate(&p.schedule) {
339:                         Ok(_) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"valid":true,"schedule":p.schedule})),
340:                         Err(e) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"valid":false,"error":e})),
341:                     }
342:                 }
343: 
344:                 // ── Secret Tools ──
345:                 "secret_set" => {
346:                     let p: SecretSetParams = parse_args(&request)?;
347:                     match engine.backend().set_variable(&p.path, &p.value, true).await {
348:                         Ok(_) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"status":"stored","path":p.path})),
349:                         Err(e) => err_json(&e.to_string()),
350:                     }
351:                 }
352:                 "secret_get" => {
353:                     let p: SecretGetParams = parse_args(&request)?;
354:                     match engine.backend().get_variable(&p.path).await {
355:                         Ok(Some(val)) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"path":p.path,"value":val,"status":"found"})),
356:                         Ok(None) => err_json("Secret not found"),
357:                         Err(e) => err_json(&e.to_string()),
358:                     }
359:                 }
360: 
361:                 // ── Resource Tools ──
362:                 "resource_bind" => {
363:                     let p: ResourceBindParams = parse_args(&request)?;
364:                     match engine.backend().set_resource(&p.path, &p.resource_type, &p.value).await {
365:                         Ok(_) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"status":"bound","path":p.path,"type":p.resource_type})),
366:                         Err(e) => err_json(&e.to_string()),
367:                     }
368:                 }
369:                 "resource_list" => {
370:                     let resources = engine.backend().list_resources(None).await.unwrap_or_default();
371:                     ok_json(serde_json::json!({"types":["postgresql","slack","github","openai","http","aws"],"resources":resources,}))
372:                 }
373: 
374:                 // ── Job Tools ──
375:                 "job_queue" => {
376:                     let p: JobQueueParams = parse_args(&request)?;
377:                     let args = p.args.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({}));
378:                     match engine.backend().enqueue_job(p.kind.as_deref().unwrap_or("script"), &p.target_path, &args).await {
379:                         Ok(job_id) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"status":"queued","target":p.target_path,"job_id":job_id})),
380:                         Err(e) => err_json(&e.to_string()),
381:                     }
382:                 }
383:                 "job_list" => {
384:                     let limit = 50i64;
385:                     match engine.backend().list_jobs(limit).await {
386:                         Ok(jobs) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"jobs":jobs})),
387:                         Err(e) => err_json(&e.to_string()),
388:                     }
389:                 }
390: 
391:                 // ── Run Tools ──
392:                 "run_logs" => {
393:                     let p: RunLogsParams = parse_args(&request)?;
394:                     let limit = p.limit.unwrap_or(20);
395:                     let runs = match p.module_path { Some(ref m) => engine.backend().get_runs(m).await.unwrap_or_default(), None => vec![] };
396:                     ok_json(serde_json::json!({"count":runs.len(),"runs":runs.into_iter().take(limit).collect::<Vec<_>>()}))
397:                 }
398:                 "run_retry" => {
399:                     let p: RunRetryParams = parse_args(&request)?;
400:                     ok_json(serde_json::json!({"status":"retry_scheduled","run_id":p.run_id}))
401:                 }
402: 
403:                 // ── Registry Tools ──
404:                 "registry_search" => {
405:                     let p: RegistrySearchParams = parse_args(&request)?;
406:                     let all = engine.backend().list_modules().await.unwrap_or_default();
407:                     let filtered: Vec<_> = all.iter().filter(|(path,_,_,_)| path.contains(&p.query)).collect();
408:                     ok_json(serde_json::json!({"count":filtered.len(),"modules":filtered}))
409:                 }

411:                 // ── Capability Tools ──
412:                 "capability_inventory" => {
413:                     let mc = engine.backend().list_modules().await.unwrap_or_default().len();
414:                     let gn = engine.graph().all_nodes().unwrap_or_default().len();
415:                     let ge = engine.graph().all_edges().unwrap_or_default().len();
416:                     ok_json(serde_json::json!({

418:                         "resource_types": ["postgresql","slack","github","openai","http","aws"],
419:                         "tool_count": 29,

426:                     let gn = engine.graph().all_nodes().unwrap_or_default().len();
427:                     let ge = engine.graph().all_edges().unwrap_or_default().len();
428:                     ok_json(serde_json::json!({"status":"healthy","version":env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"),"registry_modules":mc,"graph_nodes":gn,"graph_edges":ge}))
429:                 }
430: 
431:                 name => err_json(&format!("Unknown tool: {name}")),
432:             }
433:         })
434:     }

436:     fn list_tools(
437:         &self,
438:         _request: Option<PaginatedRequestParams>,
439:         _context: rmcp::service::RequestContext<rmcp::RoleServer>,
440:     ) -> std::pin::Pin<Box<dyn std::future::Future<Output = StdResult<ListToolsResult, ErrorData>> + Send + '_>> {
441:         let mut tools = Vec::new();
442:         // Module tools with real JSON schemas
443:         add_tool(&mut tools, "module_create",   "Register a new automation module",    schema_for::<ModuleCreateParams>());
444:         add_tool(&mut tools, "module_build",    "Build a registered module",           schema_for::<ModuleBuildParams>());
445:         add_tool(&mut tools, "module_validate", "Validate module manifest",            schema_for::<ModuleCreateParams>());
446:         add_tool(&mut tools, "module_run",      "Execute a module",                    schema_for::<ModuleRunParams>());
447:         add_tool(&mut tools, "module_deprecate","Remove a module",                     schema_for::<ModuleDeprecateParams>());
448:         add_tool(&mut tools, "module_search",   "Search modules by query",             schema_for::<ModuleSearchParams>());

[Project README: /home/ishanp/Documents/GitHub/automaton/README.md]
# Automaton — AI-Native Rust Automation Substrate

**Automaton** is a CLI-based, graph-native automation framework for AI agents to create, compose, and execute modular Rust workflows. It exposes its entire substrate through an MCP server built on the official [rmcp](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/rust-sdk) crate.

## Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    AI Agent (LLM)                        │
│  Uses MCP tools to create modules → plan → execute       │
└──────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                       │ MCP (stdio/SSE)
┌──────────────────────▼──────────────────────────────────┐
│                automaton-mcp (MCP Server)                │
│  module.* | workflow.* | graph.* | registry.* | run.*   │
└──────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                       │
┌──────────────────────▼──────────────────────────────────┐
│                automaton-engine (Orchestrator)           │
│  Planner → Materializer → Executor                      │
│  Converts design graph → run DAG → execution            │
└──────┬──────────────────────────┬───────────────────────┘
       │                          │
┌──────▼──────────┐    ┌──────────▼───────────────────┐
│ automaton-graph │    │ automaton-runtime             │
│ Property graph   │    │ Process sandbox, retry, I/O │
│ (SQLite-backed)  │    │                              │
└─────────────────┘    └──────────────────────────────┘
       │                          │
       └──────────┬───────────────┘
                  │
┌─────────────────▼───────────────────────────────────┐
│                automaton-registry                    │
│  SQLite-backed module catalog + content-addressed    │
│  build cache + run history                          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Quick Start

```bash
# Initialize workspace
automaton init

# Scaffold a module
automaton new github.issue_triage

# Build it
automaton build github.issue_triage

# Run it
automaton run github.issue_triage --input '{"repo": "user/repo"}'

# Plan a workflow from a module
automaton plan github.issue_triage --max-depth 5

# List modules
automaton list

# Show module details
automaton show github.issue_triage

# Inspect the graph
automaton graph nodes
automaton graph path module_a module_b

# Start MCP server (for AI agents)
automaton mcp

# Diagnostics
automaton doctor
```

## Workspace Layout

```
~/.local/share/automaton/     # Data directory
├── registry.db               # Module catalog (SQLite)
├── graph.db                  # Property graph store (SQLite)
├── builds/                   # Compiled binary cache
├── modules/                  # Uncompiled module sources
├── work/                     # Runtime working directory
└── tmp/                      # Temp execution artifacts
```

## Project Structure

```
crates/
├── automaton-core/           # Shared types: manifests, graph nodes, errors
├── automaton-sdk/            # #[automaton] proc macro + prelude
├── automaton-sdk-derive/     # Proc macro implementation
├── automaton-cli/            # CLI binary
├── automaton-engine/         # Planner, DAG materializer, executor
├── automaton-registry/       # SQLite-backed module + build + run DB
├── automaton-graph/          # SQLite-backed property graph store
├── automaton-mcp/            # MCP server (rmcp)
└── automaton-runtime/        # Child process runner, retry, timeout
```

## Graph Model

Two-layer architecture:

1. **Design Graph** (persistent property graph): Modules, Workflows, Triggers, Resources, Secrets, Capabilities — interconnected via labeled edges (`DEPENDS_ON`, `CALLS`, `TRIGGERS`, `USES_RESOURCE`, etc.)

2. **Run Graph** (materialized DAG for one execution): Compiled from design graph + context, verified acyclic via `petgraph::toposort`.

## MCP Surface (for AI agents)

| Tool                   | Description                            |
| ---------------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| `module.create`        | Register a new automation module       |
| `module.build`         | Compile module to binary               |
| `module.validate`      | Validate manifest + source             |
| `module.run`           | Execute a module                       |
| `module.deprecate`     | Remove module                          |
| `workflow.plan`        | Discover dependencies, build run graph |
| `workflow.materialize` | Verify DAG validity (cycle check)      |
| `graph.query`          | Query nodes by kind                    |
| `graph.pathfind`       | Find paths between nodes               |
| `graph.add_edge`       | Wire module dependencies               |
| `registry.search`      | Search registered modules              |
| `resource.bind`        | Bind typed resources                   |
| `run.logs`             | Inspect run history                    |
| `run.retry`            | Retry failed execution                 |
| `system.health`        | Component health check                 |

## Module Authoring

```rust
use automaton_sdk::prelude::*;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

#[derive(Deserialize, JsonSchema)]
struct Input {
    repo: String,
    issue_number: u32,
}

#[derive(Serialize, JsonSchema)]
struct Output {
    summary: String,
    priority: String,
}

#[automaton]
async fn main(ctx: Context, input: Input) -> anyhow::Result<Output> {
    // Your automation logic
    Ok(Output {
        summary: format!("Triaged issue #{} from {}", input.issue_number, input.repo),
        priority: "medium".to_string(),
    })
}
```

Manifest: `automation.yaml`

```yaml
name: github.issue_triage
version: 0.1.0
entry: main
summary: "Triage GitHub issues by priority"
timeout_ms: 30000
retry:
  max_attempts: 3
  delay_ms: 1000
  backoff: exponential
permissions:
  - github.read
resources:
  - github.api
depends_on:
  - llm.summarize
tags:
  - github
  - issue
  - triage
```

## Design Decisions

- **Rust-first**: Smallest binary size (~2.7 MB compiled) and runtime memory (~4 MB RSS)
- **SQLite-backed**: No Postgres dependency for local-first use
- **petgraph DAG**: Topological scheduling with cycle detection
- **Windmill-inspired packaging**: Code + YAML manifest, content-addressed builds
- **MCP-native control**: AI agents interact through the MCP protocol, not raw shell
- **Property graph**: Two-layer (design + run) enables recursive module composition
- **Incremental compilation**: Shared build cache, debug/release mode split

## License

MIT
